properties.remark: FUNNEL CLOUD REPORTED IN THIS AREA. LE REPORTS HOUSES DAMAGED BY POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

THE FAIR PLAY TORNADO PRODUCED EF1 DAMAGE WITH MAXIMUM WINDS UP TO 110 MPH AND A MAX WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 14 MINUTES ALONG A 8.8 MILE PATH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN TO THE SOUTHWEST OF FAIR PLAY AND TRACKED JUST TO THE WEST AND NORTH OF TOWN PRIOR TO LIFTING BEFORE HIGHWAY 13. ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO THERE WERE SEVERAL UPROOTED OR SNAPPED TREES, SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS, AND DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES INCLUDING A MANUFACTURED HOME THAT WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED.
203 HIGHWAY 32 2 BARNS DESTROYED.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 W FAIR PLAY. 203 HIGHWAY 32 2 BARNS DESTROYED ALONG WITH POWER LINES DOWN IN THIS AREA.
TORNADO OF UNKNOWN INTENSITY OBSERVED. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

THE EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN A FEW MILES SOUTHEAST OF CROSS TIMBERS ON HIGHWAY CC. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST BEFORE QUICKLY LIFTING. ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES, AND AN OUTBUILDING COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE TORNADO PRODUCED EF1 DAMAGE WITH MAXIMUM WINDS UP TO 100 MPH AND A MAX WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 3 MINUTES ALONG A 1.6 MILE PATH.

THE TORNADO PRODUCED EF1 DAMAGE WITH MAXIMUM WINDS UP TO 98 MPH AND A MIX WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 11 MINUTES ALONG A 7 MILE PATH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF SPRINGFIELD AND LIFTED JUST NORTH OF STRAFFORD. THE TORNADO RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO AN AUTOMOBILE SERVICE BUILDING, SEVERAL HOMES, AND UPROOTED OR SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES.

*** 1 FATAL *** EXCESSIVE RAIN CAUSED FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE OSAGE FORK RIVER AT AUBURN ROAD. A 53 YEAR OLD FEMALE ATTEMPTED TO DRIVE ACROSS THE FLOODED LOW WATER CROSSING AND HER VEHICLE BECAME DISABLED. THE VICTIM EXITED THE VEHICLE AND ATTEMPTED TO SWIM TO SHORE BUT WAS UNSUCCESSFUL.

ADDS TO PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 WSW OSAGE BEACH. SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO STRIP MALL. COLLABORATION BETWEEN THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OFFICIALS DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WIND ACCELERATED AS IT TRAVELLED EASTWARD ALONG A LONG FETCH OF LAKE OF THE OZARKS. THE WIND THEN EXITED THE LAKE THROUGH A SMALL COVE JUST NORTH OF GRANDGLAIZE CREEK AND LIFTED A LARGE PORTION OF ROOF OFF OF THE STRIP MALL. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
